title: rubus guilhotii
intro: Rubus guilhotii, also known as Guilhot's raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean. It is a type of edible fruit-bearing plant that belongs to the rose family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. The leaves and roots of the plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health conditions, and the fruit can be eaten fresh or used to make jams and preserves.
